ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 15, 179034, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH cleaned_amounts AS (
    SELECT
        *,
        CASE
            WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
            ELSE
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), E'^[\\$€£]+', '', 'g'),
                        E'^(?:Euro|Dollar|EUR|USD|GBP|CHF)\\s+', '', 'gi'
                    ),
                    E'[\\s]', ''
                )
        END AS raw_amount
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
)
SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    name AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('qualification', 'qualifikation', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'angebot/preisanfrage') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'gewonnen') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN raw_amount IS NULL OR TRIM(raw_amount) = '' OR TRIM(raw_amount) = '0' THEN 0.0
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
            (REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'\.', '', 'g'))::DOUBLE PRECISION * 10 +
            (SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'[.]', '', 'g'), ',', -1))::DOUBLE PRECISION / 10
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d+,\d+$' THEN
            (raw_amount)::DOUBLE PRECISION
        ELSE
            CAST(raw_amount AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN currencyisocode IS NULL OR TRIM(currencyisocode) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('USD', '$', 'DOLLAR') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('EUR', 'EURO') THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('GBP', '£') THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('CHF') THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM cleaned_amounts
', tool_call_id='call_oqyuqo30', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 27, 692090, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 27, 692586, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e68aece67f', conversation_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e51f2488e9')

ModelResponse(parts=[ThinkingPart(content='The issue is in the Amount calculation. The European number format regex and handling seems off, and there might be edge cases where raw_amount has characters that can't be cast to DOUBLE PRECISION.

Let me analyze:
1. `raw_amount ~ '^\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d+$'` - this handles European format like `1.234,56`. The fix logic `(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'\.', '', 'g'))::DOUBLE PRECISION * 10 + (SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'[.]', '', 'g'), ',', -1))::DOUBLE PRECISION / 10` seems wrong. If we remove dots, `1.234,56` becomes `1234,56`. Then SPLIT_PART on `,` gives parts: `1234` and `56`. So it does `1234 * 10 + 56 / 10 = 12340 + 5.6 = 12345.6`. That's wrong - should be 1234.56.

Actually wait, let me reconsider. The formula seems to multiply by 10 which is odd. For `1.234,56`:
- Remove dots: `1234,56`
- SPLIT_PART on `,` gives: part 1 = '1234', part 2 = '56'
- The formula: `1234 * 10 + 56 / 10 = 12340 + 5.6 = 12345.6`

This is clearly wrong. It should be just `(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, '[.]', '', 'g')::TEXT || SPLIT_PART(...))` approach or more simply: `CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, '[.]', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)`.

Also the second pattern `raw_amount ~ '^\d+,\d+$'` and `(raw_amount)::DOUBLE PRECISION` won't work because European-style decimal numbers can't be directly cast to DOUBLE PRECISION - PostgreSQL expects a period as the decimal separator.

The error "invalid input syntax for type double precision: """ could also happen if raw_amount has some unexpected characters after the REGEXP_REPLACE cleanup.

Let me fix the logic to properly handle all amount formats by first normalizing to a standard format, then casting. I'll simplify the approach.

Actually, let me check what data looks like first to understand better.', id='reasoning_content', provider_name='openai'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='query_postgres', args='{"sql": "SELECT * FROM \"fixture_messy_data_v2_src\".\"opportunity\" LIMIT 20"}', tool_call_id='call_a0mktd5d')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=2610, output_tokens=605), model_name='ise-ollama/qwen3.6:35b-a3b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 49, 71312, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 49, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-721c0549-f46c-4fc5-9874-488bf6931a6a', finish_reason='tool_call')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 15, 179034, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:
overall_status: partial
- Opportunity: Database Error in model Opportunity (models/Opportunity.sql)
  invalid input syntax for type double precision: ""
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/Opportunity.sql', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 15, 179045, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 15, 179453, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e68aece67f', conversation_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e51f2488e9')

This suggests that some amount/currency field is being cast to DOUBLE PRECISION and encountering empty strings or other non-numeric values.\n\nLet me first read the Opportunity model to understand what\'s happening.', id='reasoning_content', provider_name='openai'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='read_file', args='{"path": "models/Opportunity.sql"}', tool_call_id='call_oqyuqo30')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=1580, output_tokens=89), model_name='ise-ollama/qwen3.6:35b-a3b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 27, 689580, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 27, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-bad3a8a0-180f-43dc-9098-5af8c9d38443', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e68aece67f', conversation_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e51f2488e9')

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH cleaned_amounts AS (
    SELECT
        *,
        CASE
            WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
            ELSE
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), E'^[\\$€£]+', '', 'g'),
                        E'^(?:Euro|Dollar|EUR|USD|GBP|CHF)\\s+', '', 'gi'
                    ),
                    E'[\\s]', ''
                )
        END AS raw_amount
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
)
SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    name AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('qualification', 'qualifikation', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'angebot/preisanfrage') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'gewonnen') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN raw_amount IS NULL OR TRIM(raw_amount) = '' OR TRIM(raw_amount) = '0' THEN 0.0
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
            (REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'\.', '', 'g'))::DOUBLE PRECISION * 10 +
            (SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'[.]', '', 'g'), ',', -1))::DOUBLE PRECISION / 10
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d+,\d+$' THEN
            (raw_amount)::DOUBLE PRECISION
        ELSE
            CAST(raw_amount AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN currencyisocode IS NULL OR TRIM(currencyisocode) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('USD', '$', 'DOLLAR') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('EUR', 'EURO') THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('GBP', '£') THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('CHF') THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM cleaned_amounts
', tool_call_id='call_oqyuqo30', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 27, 692090, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 27, 692586, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e68aece67f', conversation_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e51f2488e9')

ModelResponse(parts=[ThinkingPart(content='The issue is in the Amount calculation. The European number format regex and handling seems off, and there might be edge cases where raw_amount has characters that can't be cast to DOUBLE PRECISION.

Let me analyze:

The fix logic `(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'\.', '', 'g'))::DOUBLE PRECISION * 10 + (SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'[.]', '', 'g'), ',', -1))::DOUBLE PRECISION / 10` seems wrong. Also the second pattern `raw_amount ~ '^\d+,\d+$'` and `(raw_amount)::DOUBLE PRECISION` won't work because European-style decimal numbers can't be directly cast to DOUBLE PRECISION - PostgreSQL expects a period as the decimal separator.

The error "invalid input syntax for type double precision: """ could also happen if raw_amount has some unexpected characters after the REGEXP_REPLACE cleanup.

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH cleaned_amounts AS (
    SELECT
        *,
        CASE
            WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
            ELSE
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), E'^[\\$€£]+', '', 'g'),
                        E'^(?:Euro|Dollar|EUR|USD|GBP|CHF)\\s+', '', 'gi'
                    ),
                    E'[\\s]', ''
                )
        END AS raw_amount
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
)
SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    name AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('qualification', 'qualifikation', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'angebot/preisanfrage') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'gewonnen') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN raw_amount IS NULL OR TRIM(raw_amount) = '' OR TRIM(raw_amount) = '0' THEN 0.0
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
            (REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'\.', '', 'g'))::DOUBLE PRECISION * 10 +
            (SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'[.]', '', 'g'), ',', -1))::DOUBLE PRECISION / 10
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d+,\d+$' THEN
            (raw_amount)::DOUBLE PRECISION
        ELSE
            CAST(raw_amount AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN currencyisocode IS NULL OR TRIM(currencyisocode) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('USD', '$', 'DOLLAR') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('EUR', 'EURO') THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('GBP', '£') THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('CHF') THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM cleaned_amounts
', tool_call_id='call_0xyauxqu', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 58, 17, 896934, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 58, 17, 897413, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e68aece67f', conversation_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e51f2488e9')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 15, 179034, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:
overall_status: partial
- Opportunity: Database Error in model Opportunity (models/Opportunity.sql)
  invalid input syntax for type double precision: ""
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/Opportunity.sql', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 15, 179045, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 15, 179453, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e68aece67f', conversation_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e51f2488e9')

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH cleaned_amounts AS (
    SELECT
        *,
        CASE
            WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
            ELSE
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), E'^[\\$€£]+', '', 'g'),
                        E'^(?:Euro|Dollar|EUR|USD|GBP|CHF)\\s+', '', 'gi'
                    ),
                    E'[\\s]', ''
                )
        END AS raw_amount
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
)
SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    name AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('qualification', 'qualifikation', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'angebot/preisanfrage') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'gewonnen') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN raw_amount IS NULL OR TRIM(raw_amount) = '' OR TRIM(raw_amount) = '0' THEN 0.0
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
            (REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'\.', '', 'g'))::DOUBLE PRECISION * 10 +
            (SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'[.]', '', 'g'), ',', -1))::DOUBLE PRECISION / 10
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d+,\d+$' THEN
            (raw_amount)::DOUBLE PRECISION
        ELSE
            CAST(raw_amount AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN currencyisocode IS NULL OR TRIM(currencyisocode) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('USD', '$', 'DOLLAR') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('EUR', 'EURO') THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('GBP', '£') THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('CHF') THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM cleaned_amounts
', tool_call_id='call_0xyauxqu', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 58, 17, 896934, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 58, 17, 897413, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e68aece67f', conversation_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e51f2488e9')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH cleaned_amounts AS (
    SELECT
        *,
        CASE
            WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
            ELSE
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), E'^[\\$€£]+', '', 'g'),
                        E'^(?:Euro|Dollar|EUR|USD|GBP|CHF)\\s+', '', 'gi'
                    ),
                    E'[\\s]', ''
                )
        END AS raw_amount
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
)
SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    name AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('qualification', 'qualifikation', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'angebot/preisanfrage') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'gewonnen') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN raw_amount IS NULL OR TRIM(raw_amount) = '' OR TRIM(raw_amount) = '0' THEN 0.0
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
            (REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'\.', '', 'g'))::DOUBLE PRECISION * 10 +
            (SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'[.]', '', 'g'), ',', -1))::DOUBLE PRECISION / 10
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d+,\d+$' THEN
            (raw_amount)::DOUBLE PRECISION
        ELSE
            CAST(raw_amount AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN currencyisocode IS NULL OR TRIM(currencyisocode) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('USD', '$', 'DOLLAR') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('EUR', 'EURO') THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('GBP', '£') THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('CHF') THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM cleaned_amounts
', tool_call_id='call_oqyuqo30', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 27, 692090, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 27, 692586, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e68aece67f', conversation_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e51f2488e9')

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH cleaned_amounts AS (
    SELECT
        *,
        CASE
            WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
            ELSE
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), E'^[\\$€£]+', '', 'g'),
                        E'^(?:Euro|Dollar|EUR|USD|GBP|CHF)\\s+', '', 'gi'
                    ),
                    E'\\s', ''
                )
        END AS raw_amount
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
)
SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    name AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('qualification', 'qualifikation', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'angebot/preisanfrage') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'gewonnen') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN raw_amount IS NULL OR TRIM(raw_amount) = '' THEN 0.0
        WHEN raw_amount = '0' THEN 0.0
        -- European format: digits.digits,digits (e.g. 60.702,05 or 150.721,39)
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})+,\d+$' THEN
            CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'[.]', '', 'g') || '.' || SPLIT_PART(raw_amount, ',', -1) AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        -- European format: simple digits,digits (e.g. 1234,56 — no thousands dots)
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d+,\d+$' THEN
            CAST(SPLIT_PART(raw_amount, ',', 1) || '.' || SPLIT_PART(raw_amount, ',', -1) AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        -- US format with thousand separators: digits,digits,digits (e.g. 1,234,567.89)
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d{1,3}(,\d{3})+\.\d+$' THEN
            CAST(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'[,]', '', 'g') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        -- US format without thousand separators: plain digits.digits or negative
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^-?\d+\.\d+$' OR raw_amount ~ '^-?\d+$' THEN
            CAST(raw_amount AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN currencyisocode IS NULL OR TRIM(currencyisocode) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('USD', '$', 'DOLLAR') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('EUR', 'EURO') THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('GBP', '£') THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('CHF') THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM cleaned_amounts', tool_call_id='call_76x8d8ti', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 59, 6, 151886, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 59, 6, 152434, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e68aece67f', conversation_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e51f2488e9')

2. European format without thousands dots (`1234,56`): Use SPLIT_PART to get integer and decimal parts, rejoin with period. Converts `1234,56` → `1234.56`.

3. US format with commas: Remove all commas, then cast directly.

4. Plain numbers (with or without decimals, positive/negative): Cast directly.

5. Everything else falls through to NULL — including "None", empty strings, etc.

Also fixed the whitespace regex from `E'[\s]'` (which was invalid in older PostgreSQL) to `E'\\s'`.

Let me verify this will work with the sample data:

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 15, 179034, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:
overall_status: partial
- Opportunity: Database Error in model Opportunity (models/Opportunity.sql)
  invalid input syntax for type double precision: ""
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/Opportunity.sql', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 15, 179045, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 15, 179453, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e68aece67f', conversation_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e51f2488e9')

        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT

ModelResponse(parts=[ThinkingPart(content='The issue is in the Amount calculation. The European number format regex and handling seems off, and there might be edge cases where raw_amount has characters that can't be cast to DOUBLE PRECISION.

Let me analyze:
1. `raw_amount ~ '^\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d+$'` - this handles European format like `1.234,56`. The fix logic `(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'\.', '', 'g'))::DOUBLE PRECISION * 10 + (SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'[.]', '', 'g'), ',', -1))::DOUBLE PRECISION / 10` seems wrong. If we remove dots, `1.234,56` becomes `1234,56`. Then SPLIT_PART on `,` gives parts: `1234` and `56`. So it does `1234 * 10 + 56 / 10 = 12340 + 5.6 = 12345.6`. That's wrong - should be 1234.56.

Actually wait, let me reconsider. The formula seems to multiply by 10 which is odd. For `1.234,56`:
- Remove dots: `1234,56`
- SPLIT_PART on `,` gives: part 1 = '1234', part 2 = '56'
- The formula: `1234 * 10 + 56 / 10 = 12340 + 5.6 = 12345.6`

This is clearly wrong. It should be just `(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, '[.]', '', 'g')::TEXT || SPLIT_PART(...))` approach or more simply: `CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, '[.]', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)`.

Also the second pattern `raw_amount ~ '^\d+,\d+$'` and `(raw_amount)::DOUBLE PRECISION` won't work because European-style decimal numbers can't be directly cast to DOUBLE PRECISION - PostgreSQL expects a period as the decimal separator.

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH cleaned_amounts AS (
    SELECT
        *,
        CASE
            WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
            ELSE
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), E'^[\\$€£]+', '', 'g'),
                        E'^(?:Euro|Dollar|EUR|USD|GBP|CHF)\\s+', '', 'gi'
                    ),
                    E'[\\s]', ''
                )
        END AS raw_amount
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
)
SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    name AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('qualification', 'qualifikation', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'angebot/preisanfrage') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'gewonnen') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN raw_amount IS NULL OR TRIM(raw_amount) = '' OR TRIM(raw_amount) = '0' THEN 0.0
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
            (REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'\.', '', 'g'))::DOUBLE PRECISION * 10 +
            (SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'[.]', '', 'g'), ',', -1))::DOUBLE PRECISION / 10
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d+,\d+$' THEN
            (raw_amount)::DOUBLE PRECISION
        ELSE
            CAST(raw_amount AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN currencyisocode IS NULL OR TRIM(currencyisocode) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('USD', '$', 'DOLLAR') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('EUR', 'EURO') THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('GBP', '£') THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('CHF') THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM cleaned_amounts
', tool_call_id='call_0xyauxqu', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 58, 17, 896934, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 58, 17, 897413, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e68aece67f', conversation_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e51f2488e9')

        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 15, 179034, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit. Fix every failing model using valid PostgreSQL only (no TRY_CAST or invented functions). read_file each model you change. The orchestrator will re-run dbt automatically.

Errors:
overall_status: partial
- Opportunity: Database Error in model Opportunity (models/Opportunity.sql)
  invalid input syntax for type double precision: ""
  compiled code at target/run/aidmi_generated/models/Opportunity.sql', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 15, 179045, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 15, 179453, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e68aece67f', conversation_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e51f2488e9')

This suggests that some amount/currency field is being cast to DOUBLE PRECISION and encountering empty strings or other non-numeric values.\n\nLet me first read the Opportunity model to understand what\'s happening.', id='reasoning_content', provider_name='openai'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='read_file', args='{"path": "models/Opportunity.sql"}', tool_call_id='call_oqyuqo30')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=1580, output_tokens=89), model_name='ise-ollama/qwen3.6:35b-a3b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 27, 689580, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 27, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-bad3a8a0-180f-43dc-9098-5af8c9d38443', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e68aece67f', conversation_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e51f2488e9')

ModelRequest(parts=[ToolReturnPart(tool_name='read_file', content='{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH cleaned_amounts AS (
    SELECT
        *,
        CASE
            WHEN amount IS NULL OR TRIM(amount) = '' THEN NULL
            ELSE
                REGEXP_REPLACE(
                    REGEXP_REPLACE(
                        REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(amount), E'^[\\$€£]+', '', 'g'),
                        E'^(?:Euro|Dollar|EUR|USD|GBP|CHF)\\s+', '', 'gi'
                    ),
                    E'[\\s]', ''
                )
        END AS raw_amount
    FROM {{ source('fixture_messy_data_v2_src', 'opportunity') }}
)
SELECT
    CAST(id AS TEXT) AS "Id",
    name AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('prospecting', 'prospect') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('qualification', 'qualifikation', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'angebot/preisanfrage') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'gewonnen') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN closedate IS NULL OR TRIM(closedate) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'YYYY-MM-DD')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN raw_amount IS NULL OR TRIM(raw_amount) = '' OR TRIM(raw_amount) = '0' THEN 0.0
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d+$' THEN
            (REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'\.', '', 'g'))::DOUBLE PRECISION * 10 +
            (SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'[.]', '', 'g'), ',', -1))::DOUBLE PRECISION / 10
        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d+,\d+$' THEN
            (raw_amount)::DOUBLE PRECISION
        ELSE
            CAST(raw_amount AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN currencyisocode IS NULL OR TRIM(currencyisocode) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('USD', '$', 'DOLLAR') THEN 'USD'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('EUR', 'EURO') THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('GBP', '£') THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode)) IN ('CHF') THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(currencyisocode))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    accountid AS "AccountId",
    id AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    CURRENT_DATE::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM cleaned_amounts
', tool_call_id='call_oqyuqo30', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 27, 692090, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 27, 692586, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e68aece67f', conversation_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e51f2488e9')

ModelResponse(parts=[ThinkingPart(content='The issue is in the Amount calculation. The European number format regex and handling seems off, and there might be edge cases where raw_amount has characters that can't be cast to DOUBLE PRECISION.

Let me analyze:
1. `raw_amount ~ '^\d{1,3}(\.\d{3})*,\d+$'` - this handles European format like `1.234,56`. The fix logic `(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'\.', '', 'g'))::DOUBLE PRECISION * 10 + (SPLIT_PART(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, E'[.]', '', 'g'), ',', -1))::DOUBLE PRECISION / 10` seems wrong. If we remove dots, `1.234,56` becomes `1234,56`. Then SPLIT_PART on `,` gives parts: `1234` and `56`. So it does `1234 * 10 + 56 / 10 = 12340 + 5.6 = 12345.6`. That's wrong - should be 1234.56.

Actually wait, let me reconsider. The formula seems to multiply by 10 which is odd. For `1.234,56`:
- Remove dots: `1234,56`
- SPLIT_PART on `,` gives: part 1 = '1234', part 2 = '56'
- The formula: `1234 * 10 + 56 / 10 = 12340 + 5.6 = 12345.6`

This is clearly wrong. It should be just `(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, '[.]', '', 'g')::TEXT || SPLIT_PART(...))` approach or more simply: `CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(raw_amount, '[.]', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)`.

Also the second pattern `raw_amount ~ '^\d+,\d+$'` and `(raw_amount)::DOUBLE PRECISION` won't work because European-style decimal numbers can't be directly cast to DOUBLE PRECISION - PostgreSQL expects a period as the decimal separator.

The error "invalid input syntax for type double precision: """ could also happen if raw_amount has some unexpected characters after the REGEXP_REPLACE cleanup.

Let me fix the logic to properly handle all amount formats by first normalizing to a standard format, then casting. I'll simplify the approach.

Actually, let me check what data looks like first to understand better.', id='reasoning_content', provider_name='openai'), ToolCallPart(tool_name='query_postgres', args='{"sql": "SELECT * FROM \"fixture_messy_data_v2_src\".\"opportunity\" LIMIT 20"}', tool_call_id='call_a0mktd5d')], usage=RequestUsage(input_tokens=2610, output_tokens=605), model_name='ise-ollama/qwen3.6:35b-a3b', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 49, 71312, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), provider_name='openai', provider_url='http://localhost:4000/v1/', provider_details={'finish_reason': 'tool_calls', 'timestamp': datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 49, tzinfo=TzInfo(0))}, provider_response_id='chatcmpl-721c0549-f46c-4fc5-9874-488bf6931a6a', finish_reason='tool_call', run_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e68aece67f', conversation_id='019f4dd1-fca9-7754-b278-36e51f2488e9')

)

        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'angebot/preisanfrage') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) = 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed won', 'gewonnen') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(stagename)) IN ('closed lost', 'lost', 'verloren', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL

        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN closedate ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(closedate, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT

        WHEN raw_amount ~ '^\d+,\d+$' THEN

        ELSE
            CAST(raw_amount AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
    END AS "Amount",

    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM cleaned_amounts

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a senior data engineer producing a dbt project on disk.

You have these tools:
- write_file(path, content) — write a file under the dbt project dir
- read_file(path) — read a file you've written
- (optionally) query_postgres(sql) — run a read-only SELECT against staging

Layout you must produce:
- models/<target_table>.sql — one per target table (paths are relative to the dbt project root, not dbt_project/models/)
- models/sources.yml — declare every source used by your models

Raw source data lives in the physical Postgres source schema shown in context. Transformed dbt models materialize into a per-run output schema at run time.

## dbt project rules

- Use `{{ config(materialized='table') }}` at the top of each model.
- Use `{{ source('<source_slug>', '<table>') }}` where `<source_slug>` is the dbt source slug from context (shown in the prompt).
- Declare sources in `sources.yml` with `schema: "<source_schema>"` as a YAML string — the physical Postgres schema where raw source tables live (from context). Do not point sources at `{{ target.schema }}`; that is the per-run output schema.
- Map each target table from source tables only. Do NOT use `{{ ref('OtherModel') }}` or join to other target models during initial writes — sibling models may not exist yet. Resolve relationships via source keys and joins.
- One model file per target table under `models/`.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.

Stop when the dbt project is complete.

## Self-correction

- Write complete, valid PostgreSQL dbt models before finishing — do not rely on mid-run dbt checks.
- The orchestrator runs dbt after you stop; if models fail, it will ask you to fix SQL in a follow-up pass.
- When you change one model (e.g. casting or date logic), apply the same fix to every model that uses the same pattern.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 10, 20, 57, 15, 179034, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='dbt run failed after your last edit.
